metadata.title: [RUNNINGMAN] He added the flavoring while people weren't looking. (ENGSUB)

transcript.text: Look at this, So Min.
This is what generates the bad smell.
- This one. - What's that?
It has giblets in here.
- Here. Do you see it? - Yes.
I should get rid of this.
(Skilled)
When I was working at a chicken restaurant...
Did you work at a chicken restaurant?
- I worked at KFC. - Really?
- Do you know the taste? - I do.
Can you make fried chicken like them?
- Of course. - I want that.
Okay.
Look. This one looks delicious.
(He is to cook 4 chickens today.)
(Cut the chicken in half and make cuts so the salt will penetrate.)
I should make cuts.
(Cut the chicken in half and make cuts so the salt will penetrate.)
This must be delicious.
(Season it with salt and pepper.)
- Nice. - I'm done seasoning it.
Hang on.
Nine.
(He finishes making the batter. The chicken is ready to be fried.)
The chicken is ready.
Once the oil is ready, I can fry it.
Gosh, this is beautiful.
(Where's Seok Jin?)
- Hey, isn't this cabbage pretty? - What's that?
Look at this.
- You can't do that. - You should remove the leaves.
I can do that here.
You should do that in the patch.
- This looks beautiful. - Remove the leaves there.
Give me the knife. Leave it there.
- Look. This is how a cabbage looks. - I mean...
- We should remove the outer leaves. - We should.
Give me that. Put it down.
Just tear them off like this.
- Hang on. - Tear them off.
What's with the cabbage?
- This is how it looks. - This is how a cabbage looks.
I've never seen it before.
What we buy at a market is what's inside.
This is amazing.
This is it.
This is unbelievable.
(While the mollycoddle is impressed by the cabbage...)
The fire is too strong. Is it okay?
The flame is too strong. This is not good.
I think we should put in some water.
- Otherwise, it will burn the pot. - I know.
- This is... - So Min, get me some water.
Give me some water.
(He wants to cool down the pots.)
Here.
- I want to pour it on her. - I know.
Actually, I was trying to lure you.
(They found a good target to tease.)
All right. Let's give it a try.
(So Min is busy washing the abalones.)
I should pour some water in mine.
- Here. - Okay.
(Spilling)
(She gets doused with water.)
What?
(Chuckling)
What is it?
Gosh, sorry.
- I'm really sorry. - Be careful.
This one.
- Be careful. - This one.
(Spilling again)
Put some water in mine.
Ji Hyo, do you need water?
(He's getting bolder and bolder.)
Let me do it.
(He's getting bolder and bolder.)
(He pisses So Min off instead of cooling down the pots.)
All right. You do it.
(He pisses So Min off instead of cooling down the pots.)
- So Min, come on. - Jong Kook, I need some water.
Okay.
- Gosh. - Oh, dear. Gosh.
(She attacks him with the hose.)
What?
- What happened? - Haha.
- I need water to wash the abalones. - I saw it, Haha.
- She did that on purpose. - She did, right?
- No. - So Min, why did you do that?
- No, Haha. I needed water... - She intended it.
- to wash the abalones. - Is that why?
- That's enough. - Seriously...
I'm serious about this.
I need water. I'm serious.
(He finds out So Min hasn't washed the seafood yet.)
We should remove the entrails.
(She hasn't washed them yet.)
It's done. Hang on.
So Min.
- Yes. - You need a knife.
So Min, you should remove the entrails.
I haven't touched them yet.
So Min.
I'm washing the abalones.
(She's been washing abalones for 30 minutes.)
So Min.
- Yes. - Get me a bowl.
Haha, get him a bowl.
So Min.
Haha.
- What? - So Min.
A bowl.
- So Min. - Yes, Jae Seok.
You answer me, but you never listen to me.
Hey, you're washing the abalones all day.
- Don't do that. - Can I place them here?
- Sure. - Or maybe here.
So Min, here.
- Do you know how to clean squid? - Like peeling off the skin?
No, you should also take out the insides as well.
- Like this. - How do you peel shrimps?
- So Min. - Yes?
- Clean the squid. - We must peel the shrimp first.
Are you kidding me?
(She peels the shrimp which doesn't need to be done.)
(Problem child So Min won't listen.)
So Min, aren't you going to clean the squid?
I'm peeling the shrimp.
Why won't you do as asked?
You peeled three shrimps...
while I cleaned all the squid.
- Jae Seok, count again. - Where should I put these?
- There are more than three. - She's too slow.
We need to peel the shrimps anyway.
(Meanwhile...)
(The big name in the broth business makes anchovy broth packets.)
Guys, look. Ji Hyo's going to make the broth.
We need her on a short leash.
(She already emptied it.)
Let's make the broth first.
The broth packets... Don't add in too many of them.
- I made this much. - Ji Hyo, wait.
(Both hands are full.)
Give it here.
I'll take four.
- Let's add one more. - Why?
- Here. - That's too much.
This is more than enough.
Isn't four a lot as well?
(He dunks in four broth packets filled with anchovies.)
Is this for the broth?
- It's for the broth. - It's green onion.
Are you sure?
Is this for the broth?
- Yes. - Is it for the broth?
- That's a lot. - Should I add them all in?
Shouldn't we cut off the ends first?
- Are you kidding me? - Aren't we making the broth?
(Why the green onions turned out like rags.)
(He gets ready to put in the work.)
(He cleans the green onions even though they can be cut off.)
(He scrubs the roots with force.)
(Save me...)
(A disaster)
- Is this for the broth? - No, it isn't.
Really?
- For now... - No one's on the same page.
Let's toast the green onion in the oil.
Are you serious?
We need to start with that.
- What about the broth? - What?
- Why are we making the broth? - Let's make it here instead.
Why do we need a broth?
- We need it. - Add the water later.
The water should be added later.
But we're boiling the water right now.
- You're making a broth? - Why did we need one?
Who told you to make a broth?
Didn't someone order it?
Who told you to make a broth?
- Wait. - Hold on.
- When people mentioned the broth... - Yes?
You must've heard it.
I didn't hear anything. Why are we making a broth?
Who made the broth when we don't need it?
Who started making the broth?
- Ji Hyo. - Why did you make the broth?
Why else would there have been anchovies?
That's not it. We're making jjamppong.
Is making a broth your life's mission or something?
- Just because we had anchovies? - Really?
Let's do this instead.
Just like you said, let's heat up the oil...
- on that end. - Right.
- We'll then add the broth... - Later on?
- Sure thing. - That's better.
- Let's do that. - Okay.
- Next time, Ji Hyo... - Yes?
Get our consent before making the broth.
Got it. Sorry about that.
- I'm obsessed with broth. - Why are you obsessed with it?
- I'm obsessed with broth. - I can't wait for this.
I haven't made jjamppong on a show...
since my days on "Family Outing."
- There should be a sizzle. - This is perfect.
- This is it. - Add the green onions.
- Nice. - Grill it on low heat.
- Give it here. - Green onions.
Add the garlic.
Okay, good.
I don't know why...
- but I have high hopes for this. - I agree.
You can already smell the deliciousness.
- It's not much but... - Okay, next.
- Bring the seafood. - Cut the squid for me.
- What I said... - Be ready with the squid!
(Be ready with the squid!)
- The squid? Give it here. - Should I cut them?
- So Min. - Cut them with scissors.
When will you be done at that rate?
The squid... Be ready with the seafood!
Standby!
Be ready with the seafood!
The seafood!
- Who... - Standby!
Who made you the head chef?
The seafood. Hey!
- The seafood. Hey! - Just bring them to him.
- Hey! - Go ahead.
- Here I come. - Be ready with the seafood.
That's it. Dunk them in.
Nice.
There's another plate.
Nice.
Didn't I tell you to cut the squid?
Why didn't you cut the squid?
Who added in the squid without cutting it?
(The culprit slowly takes cover.)
- Who did that? - We cut them all up.
- Give me the tongs. - Scissors.
This should've been cut.
You must've missed this one.
Yes, I missed it.
The squid should've been cut.
Bring it here.
The squid was found dead. So Min!
Were you in charge of the squid?
It was your job.
(Shakes head)
It should be added right now. Cook it with chili powder.
Standby!
Should I bring the chili powder?
Are you creating a piece of art or something?
I'm sorry to ask, but is this a performance?
Even chefs need to perform.
- Even chefs need to perform. - Here's the chili powder.
- Standby. Add it in. - Who has the cabbage?
- Tell me to stop. - Stop!
- That's not enough. - Add in a bit more.
Let's see how it is. Okay, we're good.
(Raining chili powder)
Gosh, this looks good.
- This looks delicious. - Standby!
Be ready with the cabbage!
Gosh.
(Seok Sam cuts the cabbage.)
- There we go. - Good.
Nice. Good job, guys.
- Nice, So Min. - It smells like jjamppong.
- Can't you smell it? - Yes, I can.
- The cabbage. - Should I add the broth?
- Right here. - A little at a time.
(In goes the cabbage)
Pour it in. We're good.
- This... - Doesn't it smell like jjamppong?
It smells like jjamppong, and I'm fascinated.
Gosh, this is crazy.
Goodness, the smell.
- I have the Korean style soy sauce. - Standby.
- Add it in. - Tell me when to stop.
(Soy sauce and...)
Just pour it in.
- It smells like jjamppong. - It seems to be drying up.
- We need more broth. - Add in more broth.
- Where is it? - We need more chili powder.
- And the oyster sauce? - I added some in.
Should I add more chili powder?
Hold on. Let me taste it first.
(Director Yu tastes it for the first time.)
For the first time in my life,
I'll make something without ramyeon seasoning.
(The magical ramyeon seasoning...)
(was his go-to method when cooking on "Family Outing.")
We can always add ramyeon seasoning.
Of course. That makes everything better.
Yes.
- We need to add ramyeon seasoning. - Yes, the seasoning.
(It was the ace up his sleeve every time he cooked.)
For the first time in my life,
I'll make something without ramyeon seasoning.
Can you taste it?
Yes, check how it is.
Smash them a bit.
Why? Is it too spicy?
- It's too spicy. - Boil it more...
and add some oyster sauce.
(Coughing)
It's too spicy.
(It's spicy with no other flavors.)
- What's wrong? - It doesn't have a kick.
- We can boil it down. - It's...
- It's a bit bland. - Something's missing.
It's not bad, but...
- Something's lacking. - We should keep boiling it.
Is the broth not rich enough?
It needs more oyster sauce.
Should I add more soy sauce?
- No, oyster sauce. - Oyster sauce. Add some in.
Is jjamppong seasoned with oyster sauce?
- Yes. - Let's see.
Jae Seok, are you sure about adding more of that?
- Of course. - Oyster sauce?
- Yes. - Then...
He added more oyster sauce.
Let's add some pepper.
- Yes, the pepper. - Guys, So Min...
Add that in.
- The cabbage. - Are we done with the broth?
(Pepper is added mercilessly.)
(What are we seeing?)
(Sweet, salty, and spicy seasoning were all added in at once.)
(The dalgona jjajang was a huge failure.)
Go ahead and taste it.
- Did you add pepper? - Yes, I did.
Let's give it a taste.
(How does it taste?)
Hey, we're almost there.
We're... My gosh. It's done.
We got this. Check it out.
(Does it taste like jjamppong?)
Hey, it's good.
- Jae Seok. - Isn't it done?
You should open a restaurant.
Jae Seok. You should open a restaurant.
(It's so good!)
- It's good. - What did you do to it?
- Isn't it good? - Jae Seok.
We kept on boiling it, and the flavors came through.
- Really? - Yes.
Jae Seok. Cooking is a talent.
- Seok Jin... - Recipe or not,
- adding ingredients... - One must have the touch.
Only beginner cooks get lost along the way,
but we...
(Se Chan seems to know something.)
It's not bad but...
(After the first tasting)
- Are you sure? - This...
It'll taste good with two packets of ramyeon seasoning.
But you said you would cook without it.
- But with two packets... - This is all that we need.
- How about some flavoring? - There's nothing but flour.
- Artificial flavoring will be nice. - Just put it in.
- We have some. - Do we?
(And thus, the mission began.)
- Isn't the jjamppong nice? - Who's going to watch the oil?
- When it cooks more. - Me!
We just need to boil it down. Should I add water?
Let's see how it tastes first.
If we add the water, it'll ruin the balance.
(He added the flavoring while people weren't looking.)
Let's taste this first.
- Let's boil it down. - Remove the lid.
- Hold on. Where's the bowl? - Haha.
- Boil it down. - Hold on a second.
(The staff only figured it out while editing the footage.)
- This... - We...
I just pulled off something incredible.
The jjamppong is astonishing.
- Doesn't it have a rich broth? - Yes.
Check this out. The jjamppong's ready!
The jjamppong's ready.
(This is what we're talking about.)
Careful, guys. Don't get burned.
Be careful. There's nothing we can do about it though.
- Here we go. - Go.
- All right. - Be careful.
(The boy from Kentucky pours in the oil without hesitation.)
- We need a lot of oil, right? - Hey, it's almost done.
- It's better. We're almost there. - This is like a big party.
- Good. It's done. - We need to increase the heat.
Right. Please make the fire stronger.
Is that enough? Okay.
(Thanks to the strong fire, the oil got heated quickly.)
(Part-timer Yang checks the temperature of the oil.)
Isn't it floating up?
I think it's ready. Doesn't this mean it's hot enough?
- It's floating up now. Hey. - Good. It's floating up.
Bring the chicken.
- The oil is ready now. - We'll put in the chicken now.
- It's ready. I'm so excited. - Get ready to record the sound.
- Okay. He'll put it in. Hey. - Why won't you come close?
- You should record the sound. - Hey, wait. Are you...
- Are you doing it right? - Yes, this is how it's done.
- That's good. - There you go.
- Be careful. - We'll put it in now!
Here we go.
- I hope we'll hear a loud sizzle. - Everyone, be quiet.
The cameraman will film this. We should record the sound too.
(This is the perfect frying sound that we're all expecting to hear.)
- Okay. Put it in. - Shall I?
- And be careful... - Hey, did you...
- Be careful not to get burned. - Okay.
Did you put it thoroughly on the inside too?
Yes.
(Quiet)
That's weird. Isn't it supposed to make a sizzling sound?
Don't worry. It's all good.
That's weird. Isn't it supposed to make a sizzling sound?
Don't worry. It's all good. It's okay, Seok Jin.
- It's good enough. - It usually makes a noise.
- No. It's okay. - Let's put them in, one at a time.
- One at a time? - Wait for a little while.
- No. We can put in a few at once. - We have enough oil.
But they might stick to each other.
- Then we can put them apart. - If we...
- We can just put them apart. - Seriously.
- You're being unreasonable. - It's not like that.
I think the oil is hot enough.
- We should put in another one now. - Well... It's...
I'm so excited about cast iron pot fried chicken.
- I see bubbles now. It's ready. - That's good.
(Sizzling)
- We just have to keep them apart. - Yes. Keep them apart.
- Exactly. That'll do. Good. - Are you done? Okay.
- Here we go. - Great. Put it in. Oh, my.
- I'll put it here. Done. - Okay.
- Oh, my. - Good job.
That's good.
- It looks perfect. - It looks so delicious.
After frying the chicken once, we should...
- We should take them out. - Then we should coat them...
- with batter and fry them again. - That's right.
- On a higher heat. Yes. - Right. Prepare it in advance.
It tastes so good when it's fried twice.
- I mean, fried chicken... - Should we...
add a little water? The broth might be boiled away.
- Right? - You said you removed the firewood.
- Yes, I did. Even so... - I see. Because the pot is hot?
- Then let's scoop it out now. - It's okay.
- Let's just eat it now. - It's okay. Don't worry.
- Start eating the jjamppong now. - Let's eat it now.
- We'll bring the chicken later. - Sounds good.
- Let's eat the jjamppong. - Hurry up and scoop it out.
Okay. Here we go.
When I scoop it out, you move the bowl to the table.
- Okay. Let's go. - Okay.
Nice. It looks good.
- Good. This will do. - Just a little bit more.
- Come and take the bowl. - Take the bowl.
- Take this. - Pass it over.
- Here you go. - I'll take it.
- Gosh. This looks so great. - Oh, my.
It looks so amazing.
I'm sure it tastes so good.
- We put in a lot of seafood. - Just a little bit more.
- Don't add too much. - That's good.
It looks nice.
Oh, my. It smells like a Chinese restaurant.
(The complete meal of jjamppong is served.)
- It looks amazing. - It's the smell of pepper.
I think we can fry them again now.
- Shall we take them out? - Yes.
- Oh, my. Look at the chicken. - It must be very hot.
- We need to remove excess oil. - I like the sound.
- You should put it here. - It's perfect.
- Hold that for me. - Hold it with both hands.
- There. And then... - That's good.
(Draining the oil)
- Are you trying to drain the oil? - Yes.
- It's best to make cuts. - Oh, should we do that?
- Then... - Will you make cuts?
Let's make some cuts, Haha.
Go ahead and do it now.
- It's a young chicken, so... Yes. - Oh, just like that?
- It's being cooked nicely. - It's probably not cooked here.
- Okay. Now... - Flip it.
- Okay. - You mean, with this side on?
- That's right. - Okay.
All right. Let's fry it again.
- Gosh, it's too hot. Oh, dear. - Oh, no. Step back.
- Step back. - I think we should use tongs.
- I think that's the right way. - Right.
- Help him use tongs. - Use tongs.
- Nice. This is so great. - Look at this.
- Listen to this sound. - We're having a party.
- Done. Gosh. - Very good.
- One more time. - Oh, my. This is unbelievable.
We became one.
I think we could open up a restaurant.
Being on TV shows is better than that, Seok Jin.
No. My tour will be titled "Seok Jin's Kitchen,"
just like the TV show, "Jinny's Kitchen."
We should fry them until the outside turns...
- Golden brown. - brown like bread.
Until the color becomes like bread.
- It should be cooked to the core. - Right.
- That's the key point. - Yes, that's the key point.
